Friend,

**I 'm running for President of the United States!**

_I believe we live in the greatest country on God 's green Earth, Friend.
I believe that America can do for ANYONE what she has done for me.
And I believe I am living proof that America is a Land of Opportunity, not a land of oppression._

**_[If you agree with me, then I ask you to please chip in anything you can afford to help me restore faith in America and get our country back on track.](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=e49cdb2d48514490ad86f2d5e1cc8620&_e=g_O3KM1kHycRhF-0nruPQT6EGBLyFSP_YhGtb5OKWyzThER5T7-zLzZj2vnWD6KPwnRkRd0LhLQmygitHebQ01fiMNA5PHbZrFTzqfx8ZuA8DOiDb8qYWVq3ZaBivW71tGyWCR7X4Mo6BzVzP968rK9SCWV9D1J74E41G-s73_L5ZMtCj7SlINqkvMI8SNqGpnWXwTGbL4W5FGau2F0pFrBX6oy6UTaMt5HWd5o6ZCtRlPAY9gupyc7HNzBmSHq-3DUGcdfEs-2tqoSYbFszkUxNRrWK1V7mEKwA-R4G4uqEJ-6lBBEIrLAO134KBIFAV8aVo2ig_XyaLtzmvxR-LPVLah9aD2mTKoptcmzCBgdAItPUdbuVFOIzVvK_axubbd9cBy1lfN8s6mAEl0s0lY7zUN_oTpt2wjPi0KN_C-gax2Fcv1TQpc6vs3zY1bWS_9m1r262dUOPAWTyyGTEaA%3D%3D "If you agree with me, then I ask you to please chip in anything you can afford to help me restore faith in America and get our country back on track.")_**

No longer will we allow our kids to grow up in a culture **where everyone is a victim.**
No longer will we allow our kids to be **indoctrinated,** able bodied people to **avoid work,** and violent criminals to **roam our streets with no repercussions.**
No longer will we ignore the **necessity of individual responsibility.**

It is time for us to restore hope, create opportunities, and protect America. **It is time for us to be proud to be Americans and proud of our president.**

**_[That's why I announced I am running for President of the United States.](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=e49cdb2d48514490ad86f2d5e1cc8620&_e=g_O3KM1kHycRhF-0nruPQT6EGBLyFSP_YhGtb5OKWyzThER5T7-zLzZj2vnWD6KPUmsV6SgKBRX48p6s1XFwM8tkNhpUslWhzflscPr-4CRLWBGB-bBFxp0SOoTJR1Uss3dxGCdpkwudRWPdW7ktWVN6i9Vd21Oy1BW5z5clKnx04HW8SZQ6aN4bhsGBzUo6tUE7kce3CCo6AgdPBuNE9O-uMCkYABj07SCqrC__95KRSwazRzk0mRggSejZnb4w1Dj_g0YqezgCLbCc-2oMyciXKn2gZjUbp_tp9S4aUsfrVRbFNvfA9xQw9nKWn2oaoSKs5jtzigV_jCYkkf3LUsLFgf8oUbW1bdtPVTuKOnvl5wAOILyRT7KiRHaa7fobmTPTA8jvdqjT6nWXptSvZERD4cb350db2RNnAQyi1lGpOVEhLolFH8P-XfMyPOxUjnzY0lUBtm7R9gQTmuiKCQ%3D%3D "That’s why I announced I am running for President of the United States.")_**

**This isn 't going to be easy.** I'll need your help to win.

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: On this day 158 years ago
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Today is a stark reminder of our collective history.
#
Peter,
On this day 158 years ago, slavery was finally ended in America. It was on June 19th, 1865 that Union soldiers landed at Galveston, Texas with news that enslaved people were now free.
Today is a stark reminder of our collective history. We've fought hard for our progress toward justice, and there's still so much work that needs to be done.
While I am deeply proud that our Democratic majorities made Juneteenth a federal holiday in 2021 and President Biden instituted the Justice40 initiative to invest in historically underserved communities, it is not enough. Congress and our country still have a long way to go to make equality real and address nearly a century of oppressive Jim Crow laws in America.
In Congress, and in my everyday commitment to serving the American people, I constantly discuss the pressing issues that have plagued our communities for far too long.**Issues such as:**
* America's for-profit health care system leaves Black Americans much more likely to be uninsured than white Americans.
* America's deeply, deeply unequal economy.
* America's housing system with devastating generational trauma, exclusionary redlining, and discriminatory financial practices that shut out people of color and fuel racial injustice.
* Ecological racism faced in countless neighborhoods that are perpetually flooded with toxic dump sites and lack of access to clean air, safe drinking water, and public lands.
* America's education system institutionalized discrimination to the extent that the schoolhouse to jailhouse pipeline begins as early as kindergarten.
* And our justice system is patently unjust.

**Every single system is connected** — all discriminatory and intertwined, threads of injustice that come together to hold people back.
That is why I am dedicated to doing the much needed work to fight for immediate and urgent legislative solutions to address each and every issue. That means we need Medicare for All, the PRO Act, a livable wage, the Housing is a Human Right Act, the College for All Act, a Green New Deal and so much more.
Now is not the time for incremental change — that's not what I came to Congress to do. I came here to set things straight and fight for what matters and what we deserve. This Juneteenth and each day moving forward, I pledge to you that I will fight for real change, in solidarity with the Black community and with all of us who believe in a better world.
Thank you for doing this work with me: with urgency, with generosity, with the belief that we can change things if we remain dedicated to our collective vision.
Thank you,
Pramila

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20230619-2611-footer&t=1&refcode2=2611_420207_-TPfoT&akid=2611%2E420207%2E-TPfoT)**

name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie-reelect-logo-outlined.png]
On this day in 1865—long after the Emancipation ProclamationTexans received word of the end of slavery in the U.S. when Maj. Gen. Gordon Granger arrived in Galveston, Texas and announced that all slaves were now free and had “absolute equality” of personal rights and property rights.
Since that day, Texans have celebrated June 19—Juneteenth—as a day of freedom, progress, and hope. Over the years, Juneteenth celebrations have taken place across the country. And today, because of the advocacy and work of many, we celebrate Juneteenth on this national holiday.
And while we celebrate all that Juneteenth represents, we also know that we still have much work to do to make the promise of this day a reality for all. Juneteenth is a celebration of our ability to do that—to advance the cause of freedom, to expand opportunity, and to make progress.
Today is a day of joy and hope and faith that, with perseverance and commitment, we can continue to build a better America for all Americans. To do so, we must know our history and commit ourselves to our ideals of freedom, equality, and opportunity for all Americans. Today and every day.
Together, we can do anything,
Lizzie Sign-off [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

name: Kelly Hall, Fairness Project
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Fairness Project The Fairness Project
body:
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65059960/412193923/765663662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzNhZWI1ZC1iMjBlLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CpC8e-caOWQU-MKLQ0r0NMyDhTo3FPO2htKfsUT1Ffw=&emci=742998ec-6a0c-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=e33aeb5d-b20e-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=561582) | Dear friend, Our team at Fairness Project is wishing you and your loved ones a joyous Juneteenth! Juneteenth marks the date that enslaved people of Galveston, TX learned they were free on June 19th, 1865, **more than two years after Lincoln’s Emancipation Proclamation.** Today is a celebration of independence, justice, and joy, but it also comes with the recognition that _Black Americans face continued inequity both openly and subvertly._ We honor the memory of those enslaved people and the history of progress with celebration and with the commitment to continuing the fight. _We will always continue to push for the justice, peace, and equity that Black Americans deserve._ It’s part of our mission to enact change through direct democracy; when you give people the choice, they choose progress. Have a safe and happy Juneteenth, Kelly Hall
Executive Director
Fairness Project

clean_body: | | Dear friend, Our team at Fairness Project is wishing you and your loved ones a joyous Juneteenth! Juneteenth marks the date that enslaved people of Galveston, TX learned they were free on June 19th, 1865, more than two years after Lincoln’s Emancipation Proclamation. Today is a celebration of independence, justice, and joy, but it also comes with the recognition that Black Americans face continued inequity both openly and subvertly. We honor the memory of those enslaved people and the history of progress with celebration and with the commitment to continuing the fight. We will always continue to push for the justice, peace, and equity that Black Americans deserve. It’s part of our mission to enact change through direct democracy; when you give people the choice, they choose progress. Have a safe and happy Juneteenth, Kelly Hall Executive Director Fairness Project Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won 31 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions to the Fairness Project support its many efforts nationwide and any focus is at the Fairness Project’s sole discretion. name: Cori Bush
subject: We need reparations now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
body:
This Juneteenth, please join the call for reparations. | |
---
Peter, Today we mark Juneteenth, a symbol of freedom deferred and a celebration of the ongoing struggle for Black liberation. **As the first activist from the movement for Black lives to be elected to Congress, I’m unafraid to call for the bold changes we deserve.** The U.S. government has a responsibility to fix the harms from centuries of enslaving stolen Africans who built this country’s wealth. We’re still living with the vestiges of slavery, with a $14 trillion Black-white wealth gap, ongoing housing discrimination, outrageous healthcare disparities, and a so-called justice system that criminalizes and brutalizes Black people simply because we’re Black. **I refuse to accept this status quo, which is why I recently introduced legislation to enact reparations for Black Americans.** [**This Juneteenth, please join the call for reparations and sign on as a grassroots co-sponsor of my Reparations Now name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Today we celebrate Juneteenth, commemorating the day when news that slavery had ended in the United States finally made it to Galveston, Texas.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65060286/412195384/1394223873?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20230619-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTE5MDJiZWYtYjIwZS1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=RjedA9aCkoKIMRYoV-L6wsqF5F1njKCJSr_YfZhAYCA=)
---
| Peter,

Today we celebrate Juneteenth, commemorating the day when news that slavery had ended in the United States finally made it to Galveston, Texas. It was a long two years after President Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation that Union soldiers delivered the news of freedom to the people of Galveston, liberating the final enslaved people in America.

In 2021, I proudly voted with my colleagues to make Juneteenth a federal holiday. We voted for this vital commemoration so that today – and generations into the future – Americans will celebrate this day of liberation, never forget about the vile history of slavery in America, and work for the cause of equality and justice in our country. There is much more work to be done for the cause of justice and in pursuit of civil rights, and today as we celebrate, we are reminded of the progress made and the work before us all.

Sincerely,
Abigail Spanberger
---

name: Felix Frisch
subject: a 24,000-mile father-son road trip
disclaimer_text: Paid for by AB PAC
body:
| | |
---
Hey there. It’s Felix Frisch – Adam Frisch’s son.
When my dad told us that he was going to run against Lauren Boebert in 2022, I did what most 16-year-olds would do: I took a year off from school to be involved in the campaign.

Technically, I went “online” for school – and yes, I definitely missed all my friends. But I wouldn’t go back and change it for the world.

My dad and I hitched a trailer to our red pickup truck and crisscrossed the entire district, taking turns at the wheel. He shook hands with voters, while I organized 130 interns virtually to make 700,000 phone calls for the campaign.

It felt like a 24,000-mile father-son road trip; just my dad, me... and the thousands of people we met at every stop along the way.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65060199/412195085/-321553144?&amounts=15,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2704-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Nzc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjljNzNhMGE0LWIyMGUtZWUxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=64qwgIAVDU1xdqN993QPln7t6w-lwqvZgjpiNh27zcw=)
The campaign was hard. My sister and I knew they’d probably make up and say a lot of false things about our dad. But hearing and seeing the lies all over TV and social media is another thing entirely.

clean_body: | | | Hey there. It’s Felix Frisch – Adam Frisch’s son. When my dad told us that he was going to run against Lauren Boebert in 2022, I did what most 16-year-olds would do: I took a year off from school to be involved in the campaign. Technically, I went “online” for school – and yes, I definitely missed all my friends. But I wouldn’t go back and change it for the world. My dad and I hitched a trailer to our red pickup truck and crisscrossed the entire district, taking turns at the wheel. He shook hands with voters, while I organized 130 interns virtually to make 700,000 phone calls for the campaign. It felt like a 24,000-mile father-son road trip; just my dad, me... and the thousands of people we met at every stop along the way. = The campaign was hard. My sister and I knew they’d probably make up and say a lot of false things about our dad. But hearing and seeing the lies all over TV and social media is another thing entirely. It was all worth it, though. We made hundreds of stops, met so many people, ate so much good food... And Dad came so close to winning and defeating Boebert! It was an experience I’ll never forget. = Now, Dad’s running again, and I know that, with your help, he will defeat Lauren Boebert this time. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Adam Frisch and American Bridge PAC will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thanks for everything you’re doing for my dad and his campaign. name: Wiley Nickel
subject: Celebrating freedom and strength
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for Congress
body:
https://www.wileynickelforcongress.com/ [https://www.wileynickelforcongress.com/]
Hey Peter,
I want to take some time today to talk about Juneteenth and why we celebrate it.
Today, we recognize and honor the significance of Juneteenth, a day that holds deep historical and cultural importance for the American people and the people of North Carolina.
Juneteenth commemorates the emancipation of enslaved African Americans in the United States. As we reflect upon the historical significance of Juneteenth, we must also acknowledge that the work toward achieving full civil rights and racial equality is far from over. While progress has been made since the abolition of slavery, the legacy of systemic racism continues to persist. We’ve seen undeniable evidence of this in recent years, from racial disparities in our criminal justice system to unequal access to quality education and economic opportunities.
In North Carolina, we have a rich history of resilience and progress in the face of adversity. From the Greensboro sit-ins during the civil rights movement to the ongoing efforts to ensure fair and equal voting rights, our state has played a vital role in shaping the fight for civil rights. Juneteenth serves as a reminder that the struggle for justice and equality is ongoing, and our responsibility is to continue pushing for meaningful change.
I’m committed to championing policies that address systemic inequalities and promote social justice. We must invest in education, job training, and economic development programs that provide equal opportunities for all North Carolinians. Additionally, we must work to strengthen and protect voting rights and reform our criminal justice system, ensuring fairness and eliminating racial biases.
I encourage you to join me in commemorating Juneteenth and reflecting on its significance. Let’s use this occasion to recommit to advancing civil rights, fostering inclusivity, and creating a more just and equitable America.
Sincerely,
Wiley Nickel

name: Eric Swalwell
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Juneteenth serves as a reminder that Black Americans continue to face inequality. |
---
_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65060470/412196116/-662322978?refcode=headerfooter-header&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZTk4N2Q1OS1iMzBlLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=V0C00sffp-R6kVVCOeEt85tJhTvA8mdMM6Lp7zCi2VI=)
Friend, Today is a celebration of independence, justice, and joy. But it also comes with the recognition that Black Americans continue to face inequality. Now as a national holiday, Juneteenth marks the date that the enslaved people of Galveston, TX learned they were free — more than _two years_ after Lincoln’s Emancipation Proclamation. And although we have come a long way in our nation’s history, people of color — especially Black Americans — continue to face economic challenges, greater health risks, and a discriminatory criminal justice system. **We can honor the history of progress today with celebration and with the commitment to continuing the fight onward.** That's exactly my commitment to you, team. All Americans deserve the protection of equal rights. So today and every day, _I advocate for the justice, peace, and equity that Black Americans deserve. _ Have a safe and happy Juneteenth, friend. — Eric Swalwell

clean_body: Juneteenth serves as a reminder that Black Americans continue to face inequality. | Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. = Friend, Today is a celebration of independence, justice, and joy. But it also comes with the recognition that Black Americans continue to face inequality. Now as a national holiday, Juneteenth marks the date that the enslaved people of Galveston, TX learned they were free — more than two years after Lincoln’s Emancipation Proclamation. And although we have come a long way in our nation’s history, people of color — especially Black Americans — continue to face economic challenges, greater health risks, and a discriminatory criminal justice system. We can honor the history of progress today with celebration and with the commitment to continuing the fight onward. That's exactly my commitment to you, team. All Americans deserve the protection of equal rights. So today and every day, I advocate for the justice, peace, and equity that Black Americans deserve. Have a safe and happy Juneteenth, friend. — Eric Swalwell | | | = = Text ERIC to 43033 to get text updates! Democrat Eric Swalwell proudly represents California's 14th Congressional District. name: =?utf-8?Q?Tracey=20Mann?=
subject: Conservative Kansas Values: Personal Responsibility
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mann for Congress, Inc
body:
Faith, Family, Freedom, Personal Responsibility | | |
---
| |
---
| Dear Kansan,

Personal responsibility is a conservative Kansas value, but Washington leftists, socialists, and progressives think that it’s the federal government’s responsibility to make all your decisions for you. We see this attack on personal responsibility in every area of American life, but here are some of the most glaring examples:

**✓ ****The Second Amendment**

_The Second Amendment is a load-bearing wall in our system of rights. If you weaken a load-bearing wall, the whole structure is compromised._ Washington Democrats are introducing bill after bill that would eliminate the ability for law-abiding citizens to own and carry firearms, and recently, President Biden penned 6 anti-second amendment executive actions in one day. We need to prioritize school safety by focusing on mental health resources, self-protection training, and funding for law enforcement and school security, all while protecting the Second Amendment.

**✓ ****Big government overreach on the farm**

The Biden Administration is waging a _war on agriculture_. President Biden vetoed the commonsense Waters of the United States (_WOTUS_) legislation sent to him by Congress, and he wants the _lesser prairie-chicken_ to be listed as an endangered species, which is a proxy war on the oil & gas industry, and which will handcuff every producer in the Midwest with burdensome red tape while they work tirelessly to keep Americans fed, fueled, and clothed.

**✓ ****Land grabs**

The federal government already owns 640 million acres of American soil – 28% of all our land. President Biden doesn’t think that’s enough, and he would like the government to own 30% of total land by 2030 for his arbitrary, and socialist, _Green New Deal_ policy aims. This would be one of the largest land grabs in history, locking up even more land under the federal government’s control. I am defending farmers, ranchers, and producers by supporting legislation that would _block 30X30**.**_

I didn’t come to Congress to be a caretaker in the slow demise of America. I came to _fight back._

**_Please join me in the fight to protect our freedoms._** You can ([Click Here](https://traceymann.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60a16ea89a176679a5f31366c&id=3b785caf96&e=a9d1410029)) or visit my website at [www.traceymann.com](https://traceymann.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60a16ea89a176679a5f31366c&id=67353e56b7&e=a9d1410029) to learn more or contribute online.

In the Fight,
Tracey Mann

P.S. I stand for Kansas, and I always will. I promise that I will do everything within my power to ensure that our kids have a bright future. Please donate today and help take our country back.

name: Ro Khanna
subject: Happy Juneteenth!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Friend,

Juneteenth is a celebration of freedom. It’s a celebration of independence. It’s a celebration of emancipation. It commemorates the day that enslaved people were declared free in Texas in 1865 – two years after President Lincoln’s emancipation proclamation.

Our newest federal holiday is a reminder of how far we’ve come as a nation and how far we still have to go to achieve a more perfect union.

One of the great Black American thinkers who has deeply shaped my values is Frederick Douglass, a former enslaved person who became a prominent leader in the fight for equality. His 1869 speech, “Composite Nation,” lays out a bold vision of a pluralistic America. His words are just as poignant, just as powerful, today as they were nearly 160 years ago:

“There are such things in the world as human rights. They rest upon no conventional foundation, but are external, universal, and indestructible. Among these, is the right of locomotion; the right of migration; the right which belongs to no particular race, but belongs alike to all and to all alike.”

We’ve made phenomenal strides through centuries of struggle, yet there is still so much more we must do. But we can do it. I believe in Frederick Douglass's vision of a composite nation, where people from every background come together to write the American story.

I believe that in our lifetime we can do what no society in human history has ever done—become a cohesive, multiracial, multiethnic democracy. If we stand up for the dignity of every person, if we bring opportunity and prosperity to communities left out, if we provide every American with healthcare, a good education, and well-paying jobs, we can truly build an America that, as Fredrick Douglass said, “belongs alike to all and to all alike.”

That’s what I’m celebrating today.

In solidarity,
Ro

clean_body: [] Friend, Juneteenth is a celebration of freedom. It’s a celebration of independence. It’s a celebration of emancipation. It commemorates the day that enslaved people were declared free in Texas in 1865 – two years after President Lincoln’s emancipation proclamation. Our newest federal holiday is a reminder of how far we’ve come as a nation and how far we still have to go to achieve a more perfect union. One of the great Black American thinkers who has deeply shaped my values is Frederick Douglass, a former enslaved person who became a prominent leader in the fight for equality. His 1869 speech, “Composite Nation,” lays out a bold vision of a pluralistic America. His words are just as poignant, just as powerful, today as they were nearly 160 years ago: “There are such things in the world as human rights. They rest upon no conventional foundation, but are external, universal, and indestructible. Among these, is the right of locomotion; the right of migration; the right which belongs to no particular race, but belongs alike to all and to all alike.” We’ve made phenomenal strides through centuries of struggle, yet there is still so much more we must do. But we can do it. I believe in Frederick Douglass's vision of a composite nation, where people from every background come together to write the American story. I believe that in our lifetime we can do what no society in human history has ever done—become a cohesive, multiracial, multiethnic democracy. If we stand up for the dignity of every person, if we bring opportunity and prosperity to communities left out, if we provide every American with healthcare, a good education, and well-paying jobs, we can truly build an America that, as Fredrick Douglass said, “belongs alike to all and to all alike.” That’s what I’m celebrating today. In solidarity, Ro DONATE [] [] [] [] info@rokhanna.com [info@rokhanna.com] Ro for Congress P.O Box 3513 Santa Clara, CA 95051 United States Paid for by Ro for Congress If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

body:
Today, we celebrate and commemorate Black freedom.

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65060837/412197461/550347785?refcode=EM_C_230619Juneteenth_N_TEAM_90do_6_NG_A_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNjOWFlYzhlLWI0MGUtZWUxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=7g5t-yrc50q2Whu9Qy0186C_4kbnps-XN6piQW_gH2U=)
| Fellow activist, happy Juneteenth!

This is the 3rd year Juneteenth has been recognized as a federal holiday, but the history of this celebration goes back 158 years.

On June 19, 1865 — two and a half years after Lincoln enacted the Emancipation Proclamation — federal troops arrived in Galveston, Texas, to enforce the liberation of the state’s more than 250,000 enslaved people.

Those who were formerly enslaved broke out in celebration of their freedom and kept this celebration over the years on the day we now celebrate as _Juneteenth._

Today, we celebrate the end of the fight for freedom from chattel slavery in the United States, and commemorate the continued fight for freedom and justice.

As a Black-led organization that centers Black, Indigenous and other people of color in our work, we honor our ancestors and follow in their footsteps as we continue the fight for voting rights and equal access to the ballot to achieve a more inclusive and reflective democracy for all. name: Gavin Newsom
subject: The truth about Red States vs. Blue States

body:
What Sean Hannity doesn’t tell his viewers...

Friends -
Want the truth? Here's the truth:
[](https://act.gavinnewsom.com/go/2694?t=1&akid=1455%2E4767699%2Ei273qx)
But that is not all.
8 of the 10 states with the highest murder rates are red and gun deaths are almost 2x as high in red states.
The Supreme Court has stripped women of their liberty and let red states replace it with mandated birth.
They ban books, silence teachers and make it harder to vote in red states.
The reason Republicans like Ron DeSantis are fanning the flames of culture wars is to distract from the fact that Florida has higher murder rates, worse education and worse health care outcomes than states like California.
That's the truth.
\- Gavin

|
# LATEST POLLING SHOWS
# CHASE IN THE LEAD

---
| According to our recent poll, I'm in the lead. But the margin of error is 5%, so too close to call.

39% Chase 32% Sturtevant 21% Ramirez 8% undecided
One thing is clear. Conservatives will not vote for woke Glen Sturtevant who voted against gun rights and sponsored the anti-life Equal Rights Amendment. We're seeing this at the doors.
What we're seeing is that Tina Ramirez is actually siphoning off conservative votes.
We've also found that some Democrats will be voting for Sturtevant. Making the race even tighter between Chase and Sturtevant. Ramirez is the spoiler in the race.
Tina Ramirez could actually cost conservatives the race. We can't let that happen.
A vote for Tina Ramirez is a vote for establishment backed former State Senator Glen Sturtevant.
If you're a conservative, you want to vote for the only candidate with a proven voting record you can trust.
Vote to re-elect Senator Amanda Chase, who has an 8 year voting record you can trust.

Conservatives will not vote for woke Glen Sturtevant who voted against gun rights and sponsored the anti-life Equal Rights Amendment. We're seeing this at the doors. What we're seeing is that Tina Ramirez is actually siphoning off conservative votes. We've also found that some Democrats will be voting for Sturtevant. Making the race even tighter between Chase and Sturtevant. Ramirez is the spoiler in the race. Tina Ramirez could actually cost conservatives the race. We can't let that happen. A vote for Tina Ramirez is a vote for establishment backed former State Senator Glen Sturtevant. If you're a conservative, you want to vote for the only candidate with a proven voting record you can trust. name: Amanda K. Edwards
subject: I’m running for Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amanda Edwards for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake-cd18-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake-cd18-email]
Hi Friend,
The news is finally here! I am running for Congress. As a native Houstonian, attorney, and former At-Large Houston Council Member, my experience will enable me to be the type of servant leader that the community needs. My ability to solve complex challenges, as well as to seize opportunities for our community makes me uniquely prepared to deliver the results the community needs. My fierce advocacy, and consistent grassroots community-based approach, combined with my ability to bring innovative solutions to the district will benefit the residents in the district tremendously.
My mother was the first person in her family to graduate from college, and it was her work ethic and the pride that she took in caring for her patients as a Physical Therapist at St. Elizabeth’s Hospital that helped inspire me to serve others first. That is where my servant leadership began.
Please help me to win this election so that I can deliver the change you deserve. You can watch our launch video by clicking below or here [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OC5HNab59Wc] .
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OC5HNab59Wc [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OC5HNab59Wc]
There will be more information to come over the next few weeks, but in the meantime please stay in touch by following us on Twitter [https://twitter.com/AKEdwardsTX] , Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/amandakedwardstx] , and Instagram [https://www.instagram.com/amandakedwardstx/] .
Click here to help us launch this campaign [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake-cd18-email]
Onward,
Amanda K. Edwards
Amanda K. Edwards has the experience and innovative vision to move Houston forward. To learn more, visit our website. [https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDGM5yj8j7wp9iTmm36KUUUjxRVs1k2ClWwooW7u7HQUXQ20_vV7WXIR0a2vN_9aawfuoQMFURdf0HB3Ltm-FAC6mS7xRC19Djsf6kePNlPzDxkle8RQk79yPECsdv0H3AVEql1F_RDzzOBlkrN8hwLTRPqiL0SN78Gzc-AyF-ND9Jmjj5Vya-HoQBOQMbpB8x4EAdkj9fxR5Db73pAdsVGoZWuI7FLjg9CahKw0yqJoi/3td/LcL_sLfvR-eH17tXI9KQyg/h8/MX4qR-D_iclOgTaB65F9F6UdLIBPWs48NRp6vo9GOBg]
Learn More [https://www.edwardsforhouston.com/]
Paid for by Amanda Edwards for Congress
PO Box 88228, Houston, Texas 77288

name: Dan Crenshaw
subject: We've reached an impasse
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
We’ve told you 1...2...3....4...5...6+ times how important these fundraising deadlines are.

| |

---
| | | Friend,

We’ve reached an impasse on House Republican fundraising.

We’ve told you 1...2...3....4...5...6+ times how important these fundraising deadlines are. Yet, here we are, pacing $5,325 behind our goals for June.

_**[If we are going to have a shot in hell to DEFEND and EXPAND our Majority, we need 254 donors reading this to chip in at least $17 RIGHT NOW.](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c26812%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=5%2bFIziv2tGJPZxRiCA0qYQ&sysid=1 "If we are going to have a
shot in hell to DEFEND and EXPAND our Majority, we need 254 donors reading this to
chip in at least $17 RIGHT NOW. ")**_

→ Majority Leader Steve Scalise has emailed and texted you
→ Kimberly Guilfoyle has emailed and texted you
→ Sean Spicer has emailed and texted you
**→** Kellyanne Conway has emailed and texted you

Look, Friend, I don’t know how else to put this. If we don’t hit our fundraising goals for June, we will be unable to make any substantial headway on our 37 seat offensive to grow our Majority in the House.

I know it can be hard to picture how the dollars you give after reading an email like this wins races. But the $17 you give right now buys campaign essentials like billboards, TV ads, posters, flyers, and door-knockers. It’s only with your help we’re able to spread our message far and wide and convince the few on-the-fence voters who will determine the outcome of these critical elections.

Do you really want to risk House Republicans' success at **DEFENDING** and **EXPANDING** our Majority?

I sure hope not.

It’s time for you to step up and help complete our mission to support House Republicans’ offensive.

He took $5 million in illegal bribes as Vice President, and he got away with it.

| |

| | | Joe Biden is corrupt.

He took $5 million in illegal bribes as Vice President, and he got away with it.

Instead of rotting in a jail cell where he belongs, Joe Biden has his feet up in the Oval Office!

But there's still something you and I can do about it, Friend. But we only have ONE SHOT at this.

**[We 've started an official GOP petition to IMPEACH BIDEN, and if we're going to make this work, we NEED 500,000 signatures ASAP. name: Hillsdale College via CIntel
subject: Your thoughts on American Education
body:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/vWVi-wzDvN

You are an informed citizen-I believe that wholeheartedly. You
love this country. You make an effort to understand what the U.S.
Constitution says

http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/oWVi-wzDvN

Please see a special message from our
sponsor:

Good day,
fellow American.

You are
an informed citizen-I believe that wholeheartedly.

You love
this country. You make an effort to understand what the U.S.
Constitution says and what it means, and you aren't afraid to
stand up for America's founding and the principles of liberty
that define our nation.

Your
leadership and knowledge are needed today…

We need
more citizens like you-informed citizens-because America is at a
turning point. Dangerous left-wing ideologies are taking hold
thanks to a decades-long campaign by progressives to attack the
Constitution and undermine liberty and patriotism.

The
country you and I love will be lost forever if we fail to restore
knowledge about the Constitution among our fellow citizens. We
can't let that happen!

That's
why I am writing to you today and asking you to be part of
Hillsdale College and our new, nationwide initiative-the CAMPAIGN
FOR INFORMED CITIZENS.

We are
the foremost educational institution committed to reaching and
teaching millions about freedom and limited government. The goal
of this new campaign is to build generations of knowledgeable
Americans who understand

WHAT
the Constitution says;
HOW
it relates to current issues and events; and
WHY
the preservation of constitutional principles is essential to
liberty.

If this
campaign is successful, we will spread knowledge about the
Constitution among millions of citizens, especially young people,
that our education system failed.

These are
young people that "educators" deliberately deceived or who just
haven't learned about a proper understanding of American history
in school.

You are
essential to the success of this effort to make America more
knowledgeable.

That's
because we're seeking 1,000 people in each state to help us teach
the Constitution and its underlying principles of liberty to
10,000,000 Americans in the coming year, and this initiative
starts with you-YOUR knowledge and YOUR insights.

And we
must do this work without ONE PENNY of federal or state taxpayer
support, even indirectly in the form of federal or state student
grants and loans. Instead, we put our trust in citizens like you
who want what's best for the country.

So
please, share your opinions by completing the National Survey on
the State of American Education today at the link below. Thank
you for reading my email!
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/61937614:WVi-wzDvN:m:3:2532013465:1A27619C1276B71808C2116C64A683A1:r

For our
children,

Bill
Gray, '01
Vice
President
National
Donor Outreach
Hillsdale
College

This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com
You were added to the system January 18, 2022.
For more information please follow the URL below:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/iWVi-wzDvN

Follow the URL below to update your preferences or opt-out:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/oWVi-wzDvN

with "Unsubscribe" as the subject line.

body:
# [](https://go.bobcasey.com/go/181?t=1&akid=171%2E552475%2EsO_gCs)
**CNN + Roll Call both put Pennsylvania on their list of U.S. Senate seats most likely to flip in 2024.**
[**Can you rush a contribution so we can defend this seat from the GOP?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20230619-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230619-1&refcode2=171_552475_sO_gCs&akid=171%2E552475%2EsO_gCs)
**[Donate Now ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20230619-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230619-1&refcode2=171_552475_sO_gCs&akid=171%2E552475%2EsO_gCs)**
* * *
Peter, we’re asking you for a $5 donation today to help us stay on pace with our June fundraising ahead of our campaign’s first FEC deadline since we launched.
The stakes could not be higher:**CNN and Roll Call both rank Pennsylvania as one of the top 10 Senate seats most likely to flip.** Republicans see this as an opening, and it’s clear that they will spend millions in our race to try and take the Senate majority.
_We cannot let them._ A GOP-controlled Senate would be a disaster for hard-working families across the Commonwealth and the country.
[**Will you make a contribution of $5 or more to our campaign ahead of our FIRST end-of-quarter deadline since we launched to ensure we have what it takes to counter massive GOP spending?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20230619-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230619-1&refcode2=171_552475_sO_gCs&akid=171%2E552475%2EsO_gCs)
**[Donate Now ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20230619-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230619-1&refcode2=171_552475_sO_gCs&akid=171%2E552475%2EsO_gCs)**
Our Senate majority is on the line, and we’ll need continued support from this team to power our campaign to victory.
Thank you for your help,
Team Casey

name: Tommy Schultz, AFC
subject: Exciting Message from the CEO
disclaimer_text: Paid for by American Federation for Children
body:
We're excited to launch our most ambitious effort yet...

| | | **
---
| | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zh23adqs4gi17j357vafrhp537f305si2k6b66f0_)
---
| | Friend, with victories in six states already, 2023 is shaping up to be a record year for school choice, and we’re not done yet.
That’s why we're excited to launch one of our most ambitious efforts yet –**we’ve pledged**** _one_********_million more dollars_****towards racking up school choice wins this year.**
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z8p1h74357jamsnbi60bcjkd1cgt8vp2ipkuqab0_)
---
[**WATCH THE VIDEO NOW >>>**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zfn9d90aj2335io94da2gpvtqmjnk35428m4uigg_)
---
This year, six states across the country have already passed massive victories for families who want to choose the best educational options for their children. name: David Trone
subject: Honoring Juneteenth!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by David Trone for Maryland
body:
$10,000 Fundraising Goal FOR the Reginald F. Lewis Museum of Maryland African American History & Culture

>> https://lewismuseum.donation.veevartapp.com/donation/view/home/individual-contributions?goback=true

Happy Juneteenth, Peter!

Today marks the liberation of the last remaining enslaved Black Americans in the United States. It is a day to celebrate freedom, acknowledge our shared history, and renew our commitment to eliminating systemic racism in all its forms.

Maryland, like many states across the nation, faces significant challenges in achieving racial equity. While we have made strides towards a more inclusive society, we must acknowledge that systemic racism continues to affect communities of color.

We see it in the income disparities between Black and white families, widened by generations of racist policies and segregation. We see it in the maternal mortality rate among Black women and discrimination in our health care and public housing systems. We see it in the educational outcomes.

The list goes on, but we can and must do something to build a more inclusive and equitable society. That’s why I’m running for Senate and supporting organizations like the Reginald F. Lewis Museum of Maryland African American History & Culture.

This Juneteenth, please pitch in whatever you're able to and help the Reginald F. Lewis Museum of Maryland African American History & Culture continue its work honoring the legacy and contributions of African Americans in Maryland and beyond.

PITCH IN >> https://lewismuseum.donation.veevartapp.com/donation/view/home/individual-contributions?goback=true

As senator, I will continue to fight in Washington for comprehensive policies to advance racial justice. I was proud to support legislation to finally recognize Juneteenth as a federal holiday, but today’s not just a celebration – it’s a call to action.

We must pass legislation such as the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act, the Black Maternal Health Momnibus Act, and other reforms that will tear down the systemic racism that still has its roots embedded in our nation.

I have hope, and I know that together, we can address these problems and more.

Thank you!

– David Trone

Paid for by David Trone for Maryland.
David Trone for Maryland
P.O. Box 83998
Gaithersburg, MD 20883-3998
United States

Joe Biden is taking his support of Tik Tok activists' topless-dancing-in-front-of-the-White-House fiasco to the next extreme **_by using presidential authority to appoint an "anti-book ban coordinator" AKA, a 'porn enforcer' in YOUR kid's school._**
---
| | [JOIN THE FIGHT—PROTECT YOUR KIDS!](http://trk.secureandprosper._Ayd1-0R3QLCK0VDEgbiOA)
---
The federal government is out of control. You can rest assured that Gov. Ron DeSantis will use his presidential authority to put an end to EVERY ONE of Biden's senseless policies that are wasting YOUR hard-earned tax dollars.**American children have been put through enough nonsense in the classroom through the mismanagement of Covid. It's time we focus on protecting them, NOT forcing sexualization into their classrooms.** The other day, Gov. DeSantis posed a simple question to Joe Biden:**_"Why do you want to have that curriculum jammed into a second-grade classroom?"_** We can't wait to hear what woke nonsense will come in response. As a father of 3 young children, Gov. DeSantis is **fed-up**. Are you?
---
| | [DONATE TODAY—STOP SEXUALIZING THE CLASSROOM](http://trk.secureandprosper._lhm8SNDuKR7Ftl9i1WA)
---
We need your help getting Biden out of the Oval Office. **_Will you help out by donating to our cause?_** We can't win this fight alone.In Freedom,
Team DeSantis

---

**Meet Ron DeSantis**
---
| | | | [](http://trk.secureandprosper._mCdBSzcTpuBBrptMDinQ)
---
| A native Floridian with blue-collar roots, Ron was raised in Dunedin by his mother, a nurse, and father, who installed cable boxes. He attended Dunedin High School and worked his way through Yale University, where he was the captain of the varsity baseball team. He also graduated with honors from Harvard Law School, where he earned a commission in the U.S. Navy as a JAG officer.

During his active-duty service, Ron deployed to Iraq in support of the SEAL mission in Fallujah, Ramadi and the rest of Al Anbar province. His military decorations include the Bronze Star Medal for Meritorious Service and the Iraq Campaign Medal.
---
Ron also served as a prosecutor in Florida, where he put child predators behind bars. He was elected to Congress in 2012 on a platform of government accountability, advocating for term limits and a balanced budget amendment.

In Congress, DeSantis spearheaded the fight to expose the weaponization of the IRS against conservatives. He also fought to hold accountable the rogue federal agencies involved in manufacturing the “Russia collusion” conspiracy theory. As a veteran, DeSantis was a champion for those who have served our country, pushing for reforms to the VA to better serve veterans and expand support for mental health.

In 2018, Ron DeSantis was elected the 46th Governor of Florida. As governor, he has fought for what’s right and taken the arrows for the people he represents. During the darkest days of 2020, when the corrupt establishment, bureaucracy, and corporate media united to force lockdowns and mandates on Americans, DeSantis stood up for freedom – even when it wasn’t popular.

Grounded in his unshakeable belief in America’s founding principles, Governor DeSantis has enacted a bold agenda to transform Florida into a blueprint for the Nation’s revival. Governor DeSantis has delivered win after win on conservative priorities:
* Saving livelihoods from job-killing lockdowns and vaccine mandates,

* Protecting citizens from the harmful impacts of Biden’s border crisis by enacting E-Verify and other bold policies against illegal immigration, such as banning sanctuary cities,

* Defending parental rights and eliminating CRT,

* Defunding DEI bureaucracy in higher ed and holding tenured professors accountable,

* Waging war on woke power-grabs like ESG and central bank digital currency,

* Enacting the strongest medical freedom laws in the nation,

* Turning Florida into a model for election integrity,

* Protecting the unborn,

* Standing up for the 2nd Amendment by making Florida a constitutional carry state,

* Expanding education freedom,

* Prohibiting land purchases by the CCP and related individuals and entities,

* Lowering prescription drug costs,

* Holding woke corporations and school unions accountable,

* Creating a universal school choice program,

* Delivering record-breaking tax relief,

* Helping families achieve the American dream of homeownership,

* Supporting law enforcement and our veterans,

* Enacting tougher penalties for drug dealers and sex offenders,

* Appointing seven conservative justices to the Florida Supreme Court,

* ...and so much more.

After leading Florida with common sense and conviction throughout his first term, DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory of nearly 20 points in 2022. He earned his second term by the largest raw vote margin in state history, winning by more than 1.5 million votes.

But history didn’t stop there:

Governor DeSantis flipped Miami-Dade County red, winning the majority-Hispanic urban county by double digits. Conventional wisdom assumed this was impossible for a Republican – as recently as 2016, Hillary Clinton won Miami-Dade County by nearly 30 points.

Governor DeSantis flipped several other blue counties red, including Palm Beach County. He won 62 out of 67 counties overall.

Compared to his first statewide election in 2018, Governor DeSantis was up +19% with Independents, up +30% with Hispanics, and up +9% with women.

Governor DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory with battle-tested leadership, a bold policy agenda, and never backing down to corporate media or special interests. As he said at his inauguration: “When the world lost its mind – when common sense suddenly became an uncommon virtue – Florida was a refuge of sanity, a citadel of freedom for our fellow Americans and even for people around the world. In captaining the ship of state, we choose to navigate the boisterous sea of liberty rather than cower in the calm docks of despotism.”

Governor DeSantis has ensured that Florida is a free and safe place to raise a family. Thanks to the governor’s leadership, Floridians enjoy a low tax burden, quality education opportunities, and a bulwark in place against the Biden administration’s overreach and various elite power grabs that seek to force people to adopt a particular ideology. Thanks to the governor’s vision, this year’s legislative session delivered the most ambitious conservative policy victories in Florida’s history.

This year, Governor DeSantis signed:
* Record tax relief

* ESG prohibitions

* Measures counteracting the influence of the CCP

* The strongest medical freedom protections in the nation.

* Reforms reducing the costs of medications by delivering transparency

* Bold higher education reforms; defunding DEI

* Improvements to our state’s infrastructure

* Tough-on-crime, law and order measures, including the death penalty for child rapists

* The strongest measures protecting Florida from Biden’s border crisis

* Reforms for paycheck protection , holding accountable wayward school unions

* Prohibitions on centralized digital currencies in Florida

No leader has accomplished more for his state than Governor DeSantis. He is now sharing his bold vision with the country, leading a movement to deliver the Great American Comeback.

Ron is married to Casey DeSantis, an Emmy Award- winning television anchor. Together, they’re the proud parents of their daughters, Madison and Mamie, and their son, Mason.
---

* I am at least eighteen years old. I am not, nor am I making this contribution on behalf of, a corporation, labor organization, national bank, foreign national without a green card, a federal contractor, or any other federally impermissible source.

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Re: To honor Juneteenth and the ongoing struggle for Black liberation, let’s push for reparations
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
For Black liberation, the U.S. government must repair and take responsibility for ongoing anti-Black harms. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/ZsYp6Jc2ATNP3MkVzvte6Pmk0F4jS6NOytihmYTOZ1f-yWw6Cj7G9e1dlkX4TL0NMWtZ9DST5cFtdsPS-4D2fKNVbxdzSbLD9T9x2URiTkAkvV0OpQQCoNHFnrXrpjTvxSW6aRbpiCIjQSvs83GY4DhgIvszuo10iTto8glnyRk7IyDCd4KCNqm1fQHtjhrVj5HRR12uwjTEwMFpIC63v32Tat08HuoYV1I0oqlhqC-O-OezjjYxPd45u5SoINkWxLJvV6o2suiRW0iQk_adhV1SwpOgDB58FmYLGY6PAAqmYtOPNUaDGiCZQHZTN7cqXfBMdAFQ-cRf8XYij9uUOMXPu_r9DW9Xvb2Y8Di53cnVPaj54DOwJyKtCZU7WoSdbfJD2Sb8vXWGOvd8dF00tQ/3x6/4dhCurvdQIemDNNWtwZH0g/h0/ukmBl4bU0PaKKMb_UMuW_U1dQPbWILfNHjZAQayRTh0)
---
Friend, Today is Juneteenth, a holiday marking the day in 1865—two and a half years after the Emancipation Proclamation—when 250,000 enslaved Black people in Texas learned that slavery was officially over. But it did not mean full Black freedom. **Unfortunately, white people throughout the country codified anti-Black racism in other ways, and our country continues to deny Black people equal rights.** In fact, the wealth gap between Black and white families continues to expand today. We need justice and full Black liberation. And that means reparations for the ongoing harms of slavery and anti-Blackness in U.S. institutions. [**To honor the ongoing fight for Black liberation this Juneteenth, please sign on now to support Rep. Cori Bush’s new Reparations Now Resolution.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnWJ6WF1TqTbT-FzKAzjyARESxW-SfCWQTxs6vavuNyX40IQvOu6hnp9OJZhUKTzObSwH_j-7ec6iljuLwvLIcFve_FThXpjInGmUKo5mPxQAvlukG5cYYnDBmJzJr5VIiSSI86MdHBg-pX7Fqxtdd9N3yQTn2h5_D4k58seAsjsnLPYU-HwtU6nKsXvIicDhte7MULrEBLXv_ExvxSvlXnxkPbb7u4XObS5IuB3nduNy-xMrL-MlmYDRrszr2rHC4Xfk1IU56LFOKsCrkDTtFcomZ_3bK1U7rjw-10ZxVeAEjVCJkVVTygYACxuBYdFmkIX2GvClfMrz9gLML0eFoS8NH3da5NVpdmBvD64xQdfQaunqDfsT2vgDvLUOp92kjAt21aMPk1O7cDE3yfEZAAoyw2kVmywH1eHi955uLiRsjzZkpebs1jRRMs9vFBwR5whH-ZuI0BahLo_F7SMatYukHmS8L8jJ0N7M3h6n9soq06KodAnN1ZBizfoPFP-zI1JvEXQOXTlekC1uDbXwibDDdFjQMU4YQT28sruv0khKldK7ov81CUvvf0hoe128o/3x6/4dhCurvdQIemDNNWtwZH0g/h1/rHJM8RgfxJ1neiJ_8dLZiuE31QUe6gx4U5xk4MJqMrs) | [SIGN NOW](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnWJ6WF1TqTbT-FzKAzjyARESxW-SfCWQTxs6vavuNyX40IQvOu6hnp9OJZhUKTzObSwH_j-7ec6iljuLwvLIcFve_FThXpjInGmUKo5mPxQAvlukG5cYYnDBmJzJr5VIiSSI86MdHBg-pX7Fqxtdd9N3yQTn2h5_D4k58seAsjsnLPYU-HwtU6nKsXvIicDhte7MULrEBLXv_ExvxSvlXnxkPbb7u4XObS5IuB3nduNy-xMrL-MlmYDRrszr2rHC4Xfk1IU56LFOKsCrkDTtFclbXn8fB4vE8oVcRiu8k-ZSDEePX3DMnxiL8kFSVdMZ_XohO7b4e5lC4dZLMRWwk-Uxsyk-C_dGLa4lcthTIb9OLLpvH_qttdriz-gxZWS0Xy5Gzh4LckB6Q4jXvpl3LrX7qcelWCYVPLwfnwyho3VgKkSUwT0Ig3d-bFtI5IbSkb8VV46MuO6JyIhjZ6dqbKn4Mn5FgoGu59xTgqu0KOvjShnlX2UqyW3Rz164EMvEGIUMCc87S1Ld7SsDk0hN4idxcF-C-8GVIT_gFllAeobMH6eTxYp5at03XwAEsl0As/3x6/4dhCurvdQIemDNNWtwZH0g/h2/uDu5LzQJJ_xt_bwax5E2V8P0cwwc0Sec6pbiKeJKQwg)
---
**Although Juneteenth marks the official end to chattel slavery in the South, it does not mark the end to systemic racism throughout the country.**
From the 1910s to the 1960s, millions of Black people moved North for job opportunities and to escape segregation and racial terror in the South—only to find oppression and discrimination in cities like Detroit, too.
Like other marginalized communities across the country, majority-Black Detroit has faced racist disenfranchisement as well as government disinvestment and discrimination in housing, infrastructure, education, banking, policing, water access, air quality, and more.
Here in Michigan, we used to have one of the highest rates of Black homeownership in the country, but in recent years we’ve seen the greatest decline in Black homeownership of any state.
I’ve fought for housing justice because redlining still exists, with Black Americans disproportionately denied fair lending rates and mortgages, regardless of income. Modern-day redlining persists in unequal car insurance rates that unjustly target my constituents, as well. **No wonder the wealth gap between Black and white families in the U.S. keeps growing.**
It’s clear: We need reparations for Black people in America.
[**Please sign if you agree: Our federal government can and must start to dismantle its continued structural racism by enacting reparations for Black Americans.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnWJ6WF1TqTbT-FzKAzjyARESxW-SfCWQTxs6vavuNyX40IQvOu6hnp9OJZhUKTzObSwH_j-7ec6iljuLwvLIcFve_FThXpjInGmUKo5mPxQAvlukG5cYYnDBmJzJr5VIiSSI86MdHBg-pX7Fqxtdd9N3yQTn2h5_D4k58seAsjsnLPYU-HwtU6nKsXvIicDhte7MULrEBLXv_ExvxSvlXnxkPbb7u4XObS5IuB3nduNy-xMrL-MlmYDRrszr2rHC4Xfk1IU56LFOKsCrkDTtFcsO0EqzoUv5IPf7rA24br04y5o1WgRbNlKIcYO5PyWq7wzVtpWwY9JmXFirBTKZN5yd4NLvsiqbAu2JzFzda2S_wrRu86Y-VM0GZAkjOx60vgWkjVaokgagklkkmsz6ujyzlSajCKttxZJKNAogVg4PSMpIDupVioFJXTBIS9cYG1SwrS0TYI2DmuMpPBuSGz4iklcqRlS2jxTeZv8zcRSWFQvYscGPyz7hNeBR-Hu2r_bLcsvU6K8DfwWlYLpesQ3ijtV_VLauZ6lMTAM6MHyDq91fUCdgaXrKuxH_OllhE/3x6/4dhCurvdQIemDNNWtwZH0g/h3/dLzwMCWX0YEI80acTq5W0p1P-d9ADJ7ZWHdwEBkgnyY)
[ADD YOUR NAME TODAY](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnWJ6WF1TqTbT-FzKAzjyARESxW-SfCWQTxs6vavuNyX40IQvOu6hnp9OJZhUKTzObSwH_j-7ec6iljuLwvLIcFve_FThXpjInGmUKo5mPxQAvlukG5cYYnDBmJzJr5VIiSSI86MdHBg-pX7Fqxtdd9N3yQTn2h5_D4k58seAsjsnLPYU-HwtU6nKsXvIicDhte7MULrEBLXv_ExvxSvlXnxkPbb7u4XObS5IuB3nduNy-xMrL-MlmYDRrszr2rHC4Xfk1IU56LFOKsCrkDTtFcBbBr7d8YzoDOSJy8h8u_YqpbzhcbNKwR1CKb7ag5c2kxibzC5XcAMwO9WToRH45fYxJAlezB4h28bvv3iauuEkVjl8OLx88TwPJ-e3ovFO6aBfI7o5RMUvVzm9knx-j122_X9E3DPh3GcvaF1kffalv5ytMATwRgsygemfzD-QBqnFHQJCaaqWW0LKz6DpEc8Txb2xi7-CzKe3JO3S611QN3GIfbTdiY-BZBWEkNLk6naA7oPmSD8Vct5M8-j0Evyo1OnPKHIgYUuuyYj6vxc495WifwenfEB2SziBSYTRY/3x6/4dhCurvdQIemDNNWtwZH0g/h4/CpZrKvtA0cne6VzzP8b2GjxVF4tBTYR_zHf06Yc_OoU)
---
Thank you for continuing to fight for justice and equity.
In solidarity,
Rashida
\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Rashida Tlaib******
Date: Sat, Jun 17, 2023
Subject: To honor Juneteenth and the ongoing struggle for Black liberation, let’s push for reparations
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
This weekend, I’m celebrating Juneteenth in my home city of Detroit, one of the most beautiful, Blackest cities in the country—where movements for civil rights and social justice are birthed.
Our former Congressman John Conyers broke ground back in 1989 when introducing legislation to establish a federal commission on reparations, and this year I’ve been honored to support new reparations legislation from my sister in service Rep. Cori Bush.
Cori Bush introduced the Reparations Now Resolution, which recognizes the federal government’s responsibility to provide reparations for the enslavement of Africans—as well as for the harm our government continues to inflict on millions of Black people.
[**Please sign on today if you agree that we need reparations now. To honor Juneteenth and the ongoing struggle for Black liberation, add your name to become a grassroots co-sponsor of Rep. Cori Bush’s Reparations Now Resolution.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnWJ6WF1TqTbT-FzKAzjyARESxW-SfCWQTxs6vavuNyX40IQvOu6hnp9OJZhUKTzObSwH_j-7ec6iljuLwvLIcFve_FThXpjInGmUKo5mPxQAvlukG5cYYnDBmJzJr5VIiSSI86MdHBg-pX7Fqxtdd9N3yQTn2h5_D4k58seAsjsnLPYU-HwtU6nKsXvIicDhte7MULrEBLXv_ExvxSvlXnxkPbb7u4XObS5IuB3nduNy-xMrL-MlmYDRrszr2rHC4Xfk1IU56LFOKsCrkDTtFcfV4GucgkOPuxZd6aBooqICA17XbxrJgMzKKW8qskqhHSw9e7Y_2bJoL48peQUWqA_qjbPkNgTaaqT1SDjIIE8iT6fnt8eNV8-pVsVgEVwoL_zf04cjHK5MTaylsnHY_4BYG0IvHV6PZ0O8oN4JpDZYxVQ-vfb-prabDdt9zgom8p8-wMSGJFr9jTtl3qv0EGg2mMmu5FVi0ZgRJ2LeR8nCVBiGIVch1vJisMOkyxjYsc6kmuIIbOtQVDDJo1cQyhHjnaOhHiLX_DgjEErEVzVVLhxRYXQb0e7Oc4inGYRvE/3x6/4dhCurvdQIemDNNWtwZH0g/h5/XCKfrVBy0r-eNs70KKHBonNR28Z0NFOnWZO4q-X-H-4)

Our country’s wealth was built on the backs of enslaved Black people, and our government must repair and take responsibility for ongoing anti-Black harms.
**Throughout her life as a Black woman, Cori Bush has personally experienced how Black people continue to bear the harms of slavery and its vestiges.**
We see this in present-day health disparities, the growing Black-white wealth gap, escalating police violence against Black communities, and so much more. Anti-Black racism is built into our country’s institutions, including our criminal legal system, housing, education, and healthcare.
**To start to heal generations of trauma and get closer to equity, we need reparations for Black people in America.**
[**Add your name to become a grassroots co-sponsor of Rep. clean_body: For Black liberation, the U.S. government must repair and take responsibility for ongoing anti-Black harms. | | | Friend, Today is Juneteenth, a holiday marking the day in 1865—two and a half years after the Emancipation Proclamation—when 250,000 enslaved Black people in Texas learned that slavery was officially over. But it did not mean full Black freedom. Unfortunately, white people throughout the country codified anti-Black racism in other ways, and our country continues to deny Black people equal rights. In fact, the wealth gap between Black and white families continues to expand today. We need justice and full Black liberation. And that means reparations for the ongoing harms of slavery and anti-Blackness in U.S. institutions. | Although Juneteenth marks the official end to chattel slavery in the South, it does not mark the end to systemic racism throughout the country. From the 1910s to the 1960s, millions of Black people moved North for job opportunities and to escape segregation and racial terror in the South—only to find oppression and discrimination in cities like Detroit, too. Like other marginalized communities across the country, majority-Black Detroit has faced racist disenfranchisement as well as government disinvestment and discrimination in housing, infrastructure, education, banking, policing, water access, air quality, and more. Here in Michigan, we used to have one of the highest rates of Black homeownership in the country, but in recent years we’ve seen the greatest decline in Black homeownership of any state. I’ve fought for housing justice because redlining still exists, with Black Americans disproportionately denied fair lending rates and mortgages, regardless of income. Modern-day redlining persists in unequal car insurance rates that unjustly target my constituents, as well. No wonder the wealth gap between Black and white families in the U.S. keeps growing. It’s clear: We need reparations for Black people in America. Thank you for continuing to fight for justice and equity. In solidarity, Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib** Date: Sat, Jun 17, 2023 Subject: To honor Juneteenth and the ongoing struggle for Black liberation, let’s push for reparations To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, This weekend, I’m celebrating Juneteenth in my home city of Detroit, one of the most beautiful, Blackest cities in the country—where movements for civil rights and social justice are birthed. Our former Congressman John Conyers broke ground back in 1989 when introducing legislation to establish a federal commission on reparations, and this year I’ve been honored to support new reparations legislation from my sister in service Rep. Cori Bush. Cori Bush introduced the Reparations Now Resolution, which recognizes the federal government’s responsibility to provide reparations for the enslavement of Africans—as well as for the harm our government continues to inflict on millions of Black people. Our country’s wealth was built on the backs of enslaved Black people, and our government must repair and take responsibility for ongoing anti-Black harms. Throughout her life as a Black woman, Cori Bush has personally experienced how Black people continue to bear the harms of slavery and its vestiges. We see this in present-day health disparities, the growing Black-white wealth gap, escalating police violence against Black communities, and so much more. Anti-Black racism is built into our country’s institutions, including our criminal legal system, housing, education, and healthcare. To start to heal generations of trauma and get closer to equity, we need reparations for Black people in America. In solidarity, Rashida * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . | | Friend,
We are excited to share a groundbreaking opportunity that can shape the course of our great nation.
Asa Hutchinson, a two-term Governor of Arkansas, a former Undersecretary of Border Security, a former member of Congress, the youngest US attorney appointed by Ronald Reagan, and now a candidate for President of the United States, is on a mission to bring a consistent conservative voice to the upcoming Republican Presidential debates in August.
To make this vision a reality, we need your support, as every single contribution counts. With your help, we can secure a spot for Asa on the debate stage and ensure a strong conservative voice is heard across the nation.
**Our goal is clear: to rally 40,000 unique individual donors from all 50 states.** This will not only enable Asa Hutchinson to qualify for the debates, but it will also signify a resounding endorsement from the American people for his vision and principles. **Will you join us in this crucial endeavor?**
Investing in the future of our country has never been more important. **By becoming one of Asa Hutchinson's first 40,000 donors, you are actively participating in shaping the path America takes.** Your contribution will help propel our campaign forward and ensure that his consistent conservative principles are represented at the highest level of political discourse.
**Join us today by making a donation, no matter the amount.** Your contribution will make a lasting impact on the success of our campaign and our ability to champion the conservative principles we hold dear.
If you have already joined the team, THANK YOU for investing in the future of America. If you have not yet made a contribution, we just have one question.

name: Valerie Foushee
subject: Commemorating Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Foushee for Congress
body:
Today, I am joining Americans across the country in celebrating Juneteenth – a commemoration of the emancipation of enslaved peoples in the United States.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65063303/412207893/964749054?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTkxMTkyNC1iYzBlLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pTmPT0AKP4QKHUN2r3r_8jO1LYjGVkWYbmZMDjpiTxM=&emci=8e819cb8-8f0c-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=81911924-bc0e-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=799895)
---
| Peter,

Today, I am joining Americans across the country in celebrating Juneteenth – a commemoration of the emancipation of enslaved peoples in the United States.

Two and a half years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed, Union troops landed in Galveston, Texas, and the last remaining slaves were set free. Juneteenth is a major milestone in the struggle for freedom and equality for all Americans.

I am proud to commemorate this momentous occasion with a deep reflection on our history, our progress, and the work still ahead of us as we strive to end systemic racism and inequality.

It has been two years since Juneteenth was made a federal holiday. This was an important step forward in recognizing a historic moment. But we must follow this with real action to address the inequity Black Americans and people of color face in this country.

In Congress, I am dedicated to fighting for justice and equity. This includes working to pass federal voting rights legislation that ends partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression, making Medicare for All a reality to ensure everyone has quality healthcare, and funding our schools so every child has access to a quality education regardless of zip code.

Today and every day, I encourage you to join me in reaffirming our commitment to this mission. The road ahead of us won’t be easy, but we are much stronger together.

I wish you a restful and happy Juneteenth!

Valerie
---

name: Deroy Murdock
subject: The key to Lincoln’s rhetorical success
body:
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills

| This sponsored message from Deroy Murdock is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zke0e11i2lk13qg8mrrm0u5e0898aa9r6pkifmo8_rp22sh2s8i66o77c9g6apr0cjh2bs)
---
| | Dear fellow American,
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Euclid.
While studying law in Springfield, Lincoln explained, he became convinced that the ability to demonstrate the truth of things was essential to the good practice of law.
Consequently, Lincoln left his job, retired to his father’s cabin, and did not return until he could “give any proposition in the six books of Euclid at sight.”
By studying Euclid, we improve our ability to reason soundly—plus, in my experience, the study of Euclid can be great fun.
That’s why Hillsdale College has produced a free online course on the topic **“Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry.”**
Through**“Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry,”** you will learn the importance of deductive reasoning as a foundation for pursuing knowledge in all fields of learning, as well as in everyday life.
---
| | **Now, the course is available in a beautiful DVD box set for viewing in your home or for use in a small group.** **** **You can receive the DVD box set as a thank-you for your tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty.**
Your tax-deductible gift will help promote our free online courses and other educational outreach efforts in this critical time for our nation. And your gift matters even more because...
Hillsdale accomplishes all its work while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.
---
| [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zoj52ulvcfn8e4tqr191goqi19hepdduq2jlhi9o_rp22sh2s8i66o77c9g6apr0cjh2bs)
---
| | That’s why we need the support of friends like you, who understand the importance of education to liberty.
Quantities of this special DVD box set are limited, so reserve your copy today. I’ve included a secure link for you to make your tax-deductible gift below:
[_**https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/dvd-mathematics-and-logic-course**_](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z38bs9pg49hmstj2m97u1l5vnbu565a3hid2n31g_rp22sh2s8i66o77c9g6apr0cjh2bs)
Thank you for partnering with Hillsdale in its mission on behalf of liberty.
Warm regards,
Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
---

Deroy Murdock
---
_625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z0nne5vo0gpn903r81trikgf3q1revgd1svqq1go_rp22sh2s8i66o77c9g6apr0cjh238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kuo72ttle5pq2d9ieuqfbdbie4rrcdljkorrbdt5eer7ashmeop79chl6ujuadf161rn9cdhmir76pr02sqr8d5hmej3fddj4iqu8p32cpgeapb24no)_ | _[manage data preferences](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zr4eoo6qb628a4er0s1p9kl27u7bnh1q6r19d738_rp22sh2s8i66o77c9g6apr0cjh2bs)_

name: Yolanda Bejarano
subject: Commemorating Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Arizona Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
| Hello friends,

On Juneteenth, a day of profound significance and reflection, I want to honor the rich history and enduring spirit of resilience within the African American community.

Juneteenth, also known as Freedom Day, reminds us of the indomitable human spirit and the relentless pursuit of justice and equality.

Let us acknowledge the struggles and sacrifices of those before us and recognize the work that remains in the pursuit of true equality. It is a day to reflect on our progress, celebrate our shared accomplishments, and recommit ourselves to the ongoing fight for justice and equality.

At the Arizona Democratic Party, we firmly believe in equality, justice, and opportunity for all. Our commitment to these values extends beyond Juneteenth. It is ingrained in our mission to build a better state where every Arizonan has access to quality education, affordable healthcare, and economic prosperity.

Thank you for your unwavering support and standing with us as we strive for a better Arizona.

Yolanda Bejarano

Chair of the Arizona Democratic Party

name: Justice via VPP
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| [Hi! Justice here from the Voter Protection Project. I want to ask you to make a contribution to help us defeat racist Republicans in 2024. Please read this entire email, and then I hope you’ll consider making a contribution:](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/47850?t=6&akid=8549%2E878179%2E1uzj9l) | [**CONTRIBUTE $5 NOW »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/47850?t=7&akid=8549%2E878179%2E1uzj9l)
---
Today is Juneteenth, a day when we recognize the end of slavery in the United States. As a Black woman born and raised in Houston, Texas – just about an hour away from where Juneteenth originated, this day means so much to me.

On this day in 1865, Major General Gordon Granger rode to Galveston, Texas to share the news that “all slaves are free” and “an “absolute equality of personal rights” for Black Americans.

**Unfortunately, we still have a long way to go to achieve “absolute equality.”**
* White supremacist violence remains a top threat according to the FBI and they are more emboldened than ever now that Trump is for President again.
* Republicans across the South are creating racist gerrymandered maps to disenfranchise Black voters.
* Far-right Republicans like Ron DeSantis are rewriting history to silence the real history of Black people in this country. And so much more...

It’s no secret – Republicans are using every racist tactic in their oppressive playbook to maintain their status quo.

The best way to stop them is by investing in Democrats who are dedicated to uplifting and protecting marginalized people.

[That’s why the Voter Protection Project is spending every penny in the off-year to build the foundation we need to replace every racist Republican with Democrats who will fight for true freedom and equality for all Americans. Will you chip in $5 to help fund our fight? »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/47850?t=8&akid=8549%2E878179%2E1uzj9l)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $5 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/47851?t=9&akid=8549%2E878179%2E1uzj9l)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/47852?t=10&akid=8549%2E878179%2E1uzj9l)

[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/47855?t=13&akid=8549%2E878179%2E1uzj9l)
In solidarity,

—Justice M.
Digital Director
The Voter Protection Project

clean_body: | | Today is Juneteenth, a day when we recognize the end of slavery in the United States. As a Black woman born and raised in Houston, Texas – just about an hour away from where Juneteenth originated, this day means so much to me. On this day in 1865, Major General Gordon Granger rode to Galveston, Texas to share the news that “all slaves are free” and “an “absolute equality of personal rights” for Black Americans. Unfortunately, we still have a long way to go to achieve “absolute equality.” White supremacist violence remains a top threat according to the FBI and they are more emboldened than ever now that Trump is for President again. Republicans across the South are creating racist gerrymandered maps to disenfranchise Black voters. Far-right Republicans like Ron DeSantis are rewriting history to silence the real history of Black people in this country. And so much more... It’s no secret – Republicans are using every racist tactic in their oppressive playbook to maintain their status quo. The best way to stop them is by investing in Democrats who are dedicated to uplifting and protecting marginalized people. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: In solidarity, —Justice M. name: Keith
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417 Keith Ellison for Attorney General
body:
Friend, On this day, June 19th, 1865, some of the last enslaved people in the United States learned they were free. Our grandparents raised us to appreciate the moment of liberation we celebrate on Juneteenth, but they also taught us to recognize that the struggle did not end in Galveston, TX that day. |
---
Friend,
On this day, June 19th, 1865, some of the last enslaved people in the United States learned they were free.
Our grandparents raised us to appreciate the moment of liberation we celebrate on Juneteenth, but they also taught us to recognize that the struggle did not end in Galveston, TX that day.
So many endured the hardship of moving on to build lives for themselves and their families, only to be met with segregation, hatred, and a discriminatory legal system dominated by the powerful white men most reluctant to accept their status as equal human beings.
Today, Juneteenth is recognized as a state holiday in Minnesota for the first time. It humbles me to think of the sacrifices our ancestors made to bring us to this point. Let’s honor their legacy by celebrating all we have accomplished yet understanding we have just as much work ahead of us to reach our goal of equality in our state and nation.
Thank you,
Keith Ellison

---

And this year, it could be even closer. This election is going to come down to reaching every single patriot, and I can’t do it without your help. | | Thank you, (R-FL) Sen. Rick Scott | | | A message for you, from Senator Rick Scott: Joe Biden’s America includes record high inflation, open borders, unlimited spending, and increasing crime. Defeating the radical Left has never been more important and it will be no easy task. Peter, with your support, I can continue to protect our Conservative values. I can’t do it without patriots like you, so Thank you for your support, Senator Rick Scott | | Paid for by Rick Scott for Florida | | Contributions to this committee are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes.
